extraction.one_line_summary: FRAILE Omar won from the early break after ALAPHILIPPE Julian's summit attack came 600m too late to close 12 seconds on the descent.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE Julian's summit attack timing failed because 600m remaining was insufficient distance to close 12s before descent recovery window.
STUYVEN Jasper faded then recovered on shallow gradient approaching summit, showing experienced riders exploit micro-terrain changes within final kilometer.
MARTIN Dan's mechanical 5km from finish on GC stage reveals teams lack deep domestique support when defending top-10 on breakaway stages.
Top three GC riders THOMAS, FROOME, DUMOULIN collaborated through finale despite competing for yellow, confirming alliance against QUINTANA threat supersedes inter-team rivalry.

rider_signals.FRAILE Omar: positive: Changed teams to Astana and immediately delivered Tour stage win, suggesting transfer unlocked form or tactical freedom
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Demonstrated explosive climbing to bridge gap but poor tactical calculation of required distance left attack 600m too late
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: Showed recovery capacity and tactical awareness on shallow gradients after initial crack, holding podium position despite being caught
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: Mechanical 5km from finish cost 1:43 and dropped him from 6th to 7th overall, exposing isolation without team support
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Lost time to top three again and commentary explicitly noted he's not having a good race at all
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Gained 5-7 seconds on top three despite being fourth overall, showing aggressive time-hunting mentality even without stage ambitions
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: negative: Lost 43 seconds in white jersey, indicating declining form or insufficient recovery from earlier Tour efforts
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: negative: Lost 48 seconds in white jersey defense, suggesting climbers without GC team support hemorrhage time on aggressive transition stages
